raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 1929 expands funding for gun violence prevention programs in California.",
    "Medi-Cal reimbursement for violence prevention programs is known for its proven effectiveness in curbing retaliation and reducing risks of further violence.",
    "Gun violence is classified as an urgent public health crisis.",
    "Violence prevention programs have been shown to be successful at reducing violence and saving lives.",
    "There have been more than 300 mass shootings in the United States this year.",
    "The number of mass shootings in 2021 nearly reached 700, an increase from 611 in 2020 and 417 in 2019.",
    "Studies indicate that up to 41% of patients treated for violent injuries are re-injured within five years.",
    "Twenty percent of patients treated for violent injuries had died within five years, according to a survey of violence victims.",
    "The phenomenon of violent re-injury is well-documented in medical literature.",
    "A systematic review of 19 studies has confirmed the prevalence of violent reinjury rates.",
    "Being a victim of violence significantly increases an individual's likelihood of engaging in violent behaviors against others.",
    "Violence prevention programs are recognized as an innovative and effective strategy for reducing violent injury recidivism.",
    "Integrating violence prevention services into hospital trauma centers is associated with large reductions in injury recidivism rates.",
    "A study of the Wrap-Around Project in San Francisco found a 50 percent reduction in historical re-injury rates among participants over ten years."
  ]
}
```
